Operator classes

  • MaximallyMonotone

  • StronglyMonotone

  • LipschitzOperator

  • Cocoercive

  • WeakMintyVariationalInequality

class autolyap.problemclass.MaximallyMonotone

Bases: OperatorInterpolationCondition

Operator interpolation condition for maximally monotone operators.

Let \((\calH,\langle\cdot,\cdot\rangle)\) be a real Hilbert space and \(G: \calH \rightrightarrows \calH\).

  • Graph:

    \(\operatorname{gra} G = \{(x,u) \in \calH \times \calH \mid u \in G(x)\}\).

  • Monotone:

    \[\langle u - v, x - y \rangle \geq 0 \quad \text{for each } (x,u),(y,v) \in \operatorname{gra} G.\]

  • Maximally monotone: It is monotone and there is no monotone operator \(\widetilde{G}: \calH \rightrightarrows \calH\) whose graph strictly contains \(\operatorname{gra} G\).

  • Interpolation inequality:

    For any \((x_{j_1},u_{j_1}),(x_{j_2},u_{j_2}) \in \operatorname{gra} G\),

\[\langle u_{j_1} - u_{j_2}, x_{j_1} - x_{j_2} \rangle \ge 0.\]

This condition has no parameters.

class autolyap.problemclass.StronglyMonotone(
mu: int | float,
)

Bases: OperatorInterpolationCondition

Operator interpolation condition for strongly and maximally monotone operators.

Let \((\calH,\langle\cdot,\cdot\rangle)\) be a real Hilbert space, \(G: \calH \rightrightarrows \calH\), and \(\mu \in \mathbb{R}_{++}\).

  • Graph:

    \(\operatorname{gra} G = \{(x,u) \in \calH \times \calH \mid u \in G(x)\}\).

  • \(\mu\)-strongly monotone:

    \[\langle u - v, x - y \rangle \geq \mu \|x - y\|^2 \quad \text{for each } (x,u),(y,v) \in \operatorname{gra} G.\]

  • Interpolation inequality:

    For any \((x_{j_1},u_{j_1}),(x_{j_2},u_{j_2}) \in \operatorname{gra} G\),

    \[\langle u_{j_1} - u_{j_2}, x_{j_1} - x_{j_2} \rangle \ge \mu \|x_{j_1} - x_{j_2}\|^2.\]

Parameters

  • mu (Union[int, float]): The strong monotonicity parameter corresponding to \(\mu\) (must be \(> 0\) and finite).

Raises

  • ValueError: If mu is not a number, \(\le 0\), or infinite.

class autolyap.problemclass.LipschitzOperator(
L: int | float,
)

Bases: OperatorInterpolationCondition

Operator interpolation condition for Lipschitz operators.

Let \((\calH,\langle\cdot,\cdot\rangle)\) be a real Hilbert space, \(G: \calH \to \calH\), and \(L \in \mathbb{R}_{++}\).

  • \(L\)-Lipschitz continuous:

    \[\|G(x) - G(y)\| \leq L \|x - y\| \quad \text{for each } x,y \in \calH.\]

  • Interpolation inequality:

    For any \(x_{j_1}, x_{j_2} \in \calH\) with \(u_{j_1} = G(x_{j_1})\) and \(u_{j_2} = G(x_{j_2})\),

    \[\|u_{j_1} - u_{j_2}\|^2 \le L^2 \|x_{j_1} - x_{j_2}\|^2.\]

Parameters

  • L (Union[int, float]): The Lipschitz parameter corresponding to \(L\) (must be \(> 0\) and finite).

Raises

  • ValueError: If L is not a number, \(\le 0\), or infinite.

class autolyap.problemclass.Cocoercive(
beta: int | float,
)

Bases: OperatorInterpolationCondition

Operator interpolation condition for cocoercive operators.

Let \((\calH,\langle\cdot,\cdot\rangle)\) be a real Hilbert space, \(G: \calH \to \calH\), and \(\beta \in \mathbb{R}_{++}\).

  • \(\beta\)-cocoercive:

    \[\langle G(x) - G(y), x - y \rangle \geq \beta \|G(x) - G(y)\|^2 \quad \text{for each } x,y \in \calH.\]

  • Interpolation inequality:

    For any \(x_{j_1}, x_{j_2} \in \calH\) with \(u_{j_1} = G(x_{j_1})\) and \(u_{j_2} = G(x_{j_2})\),

    \[\langle u_{j_1} - u_{j_2}, x_{j_1} - x_{j_2} \rangle \ge \beta \|u_{j_1} - u_{j_2}\|^2.\]

Parameters

  • beta (Union[int, float]): The cocoercivity parameter corresponding to \(\beta\) (must be \(> 0\) and finite).

Raises

  • ValueError: If beta is not a number, \(\le 0\), or infinite.

class autolyap.problemclass.WeakMintyVariationalInequality(
rho_minty: int | float,
)

Bases: OperatorInterpolationCondition

Operator interpolation condition for operators that fulfill the weak Minty variational inequality.

Let \((\calH,\langle\cdot,\cdot\rangle)\) be a real Hilbert space, \(G: \calH \rightrightarrows \calH\), and \(\rho_{\textup{minty}} \in \mathbb{R}\).

  • Weak Minty variational inequality: There exists \(x_\star \in \calH\) with \(0 \in G(x_\star)\) such that for each \((x,u) \in \operatorname{gra} G\),

    \[\langle u, x - x_\star \rangle \geq \rho_{\textup{minty}} \|u\|^2.\]

  • Interpolation inequality:

    There exists \(x_\star \in \calH\) with \(0 \in G(x_\star)\) such that for any \((x_{j_1},u_{j_1}) \in \operatorname{gra} G\),

    \[\langle u_{j_1}, x_{j_1} - x_\star \rangle \ge \rho_{\textup{minty}} \|u_{j_1}\|^2.\]

Note: When used inside InclusionProblem, AutoLyap enforces that the total number of components is exactly one (i.e., \(m = 1\)) if any component uses this condition.

Parameters

  • rho_minty (Union[int, float]): The weak MVI parameter corresponding to \(\rho_{\textup{minty}}\) (must be finite).

Raises

  • ValueError: If rho_minty is not a number or not finite.
